title: Phyllanthus keelungensis
taxonomy: Phyllanthus keelungensis is classified in the family Phyllanthaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the "leaf-flower family". This family consists of approximately 1,000 species of plants, many of which are found in tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Phyllanthus, to which Phyllanthus keelungensis belongs, is a diverse group of plants with a wide range of characteristics. The species Phyllanthus keelungensis is native to Taiwan, where it is found growing in various habitats. Phyllanthus keelungensis is considered an endemic species, meaning it is found nowhere else in the world.

intro: Phyllanthus keelungensis, also known as Keelung island spurge, is a species of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae. Research has shown that Phyllanthus keelungensis possesses various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and hepatoprotective properties, making it a potential natural remedy for liver diseases and other conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Phyllanthus keelungensis is used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to support liver health and alleviate symptoms of liver disease.
